system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

);

blocks
(

);

(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistry       on;

odeCoeffs

    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/3/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 27.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means OpenFOAM expects a `species` entry inside the `physicalProperties` dictionary. Looking at the history and the current state, the `physicalProperties` file does contain `thermoType`, `inertSpecie`, and other entries, but it is missing the `species` keyword that the chemistry/reaction system needs to know what species are present.

Looking at the reference case `counterFlowFlame2D`, the `physicalProperties` file needs to include a `species` list so that the reacting mixture can look up the species information when reading the chemistry data.

## Root Cause Analysis

In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), the `physicalProperties` file must contain a `species` entry listing all species involved in the reaction. The current `physicalProperties` file has:

```
thermoType { ... }
inertSpecie N2;
...
```

But it lacks the `species` keyword. Meanwhile, the `thermo.compressibleGas` file also has a `species` entry but it uses `multiComponentMixture` instead of `reactingMixture`. Since `reactingMixture` is deprecated in OF10, we switched to `multiComponentMixture` in `physicalProperties`, but now the chemistry subsystem cannot find the required `species` keyword.

## Proposed Solution

Add the `species` keyword to `constant/physicalProperties`. The species list should include all five species mentioned in the case: `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, `CO2`.

The updated `physicalProperties` should look like:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}

inertSpecie N2;

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);

chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;

foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";

foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";
```

Note: The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already contains its own `species` entry, so having it in `physicalProperties` as well is fine — the chemistry reader reads from whichever file the `chemistryReader` points to. But the key fix is adding `species` to `physicalProperties` itself, because in OF10 the reacting mixture looks for this entry directly in the thermophysical properties dictionary to know which species to expect.

Additionally, I want to flag that the `thermo.compressibleGas` file currently has `multiComponentMixture` with `inertSpecie N2;` and a `species` list. This should remain consistent. The `physicalProperties` file should mirror the `thermoType` and `inertSpecie` settings but add the top-level `species` entry that the chemistry subsystem requires.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Add species keyword with list containing CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2 after the inertSpecie entry'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.2 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
